gross.description: Snake Slither: Belly crawl on the carpet for 10 seconds.

fine.description: Newspaper Scrunch: Use one hand to crumple a piece of paper into a ball.

hints: Listen to the sound: ssssss. Does your body feel like a wavy snake?
Try making a big 'S' shape with your arms while you crawl!
Uncle Ryan says: You're close! Keep that belly low to the ground like Sammy.

gross.description: Bear Stomp: Stomp both feet on the floor as loud as you can for 10 seconds.

fine.description: Bear Claw: Use all five fingers to scrunch a sheet of paper into the tightest ball you can.

hints: Say bbb-eh-d real slow. Feel your lips pop open at the very start? That pop is the B sound!
Bobby stomps and his feet go BOOM! Say B like a big bear boom every time you stomp.
Aunt Kira says: Squeeze your lips together like you are holding a tiny secret, then let them pop open soft — that gentle pop is a beautiful B!

gross.description: Cat Pounce: Crouch down low and jump forward with both feet together three times.

fine.description: Cat Claws: Use your fingernails to scratch and tear five thin strips from a sheet of paper.

hints: Say kkk-a-t slow. Feel a little click way in the back of your mouth? That click is the K sound!
Kiki goes CLICK when she pounces! Say K and feel that click in the back of your mouth.
Uncle Ryan says: Try to make a click sound way in the back of your throat — that back-of-the-mouth click is your K!

gross.description: Dog Dig: Get on your hands and knees and scratch both hands on the floor as fast as you can for 10 seconds.

fine.description: Dog Bone: Roll a sheet of paper tightly into a long log shape to make a bone.

hints: Say ddd-i-g. Your tongue tip taps the top of your mouth right at the start — that tap is the D sound!
Digger taps his paws just like your tongue taps for D. Tap, tap, tap — dig that hole!
Aunt Kira says: Let your tongue tap gently behind your top teeth, like a little raindrop landing — that sweet little tap is your D sound!

gross.description: Frog Jump: Crouch down as low as you can and jump forward with both feet five times.

fine.description: Frog Legs: Fold a piece of paper in half, then fold each end back out flat to make two frog legs.

hints: Say fff-i-g. Put your top teeth on your bottom lip and blow air out — that blowy sound is F!
Freddy blows air when he jumps — FFFF! Say F while you leap and feel the air on your lip.
Uncle Ryan says: Rest your top teeth on your lip and blow — feel that air rushing out? That is a perfect F sound!

gross.description: Goat Charge: Lower your head and stomp forward three big heavy steps like a goat charging.

fine.description: Goat Horn: Roll a small piece of paper tightly from one corner into a cone shape to make a horn.

hints: Say ggg-o-t. The G sound comes from way in the back of your throat — like a little gurgle!
Gus makes a deep throat sound when he charges — grrr! Say G from the very back of your mouth.
Aunt Kira says: Feel a little rumble way in the back of your throat, like a tiny drum — that deep, warm sound is your G!

gross.description: Horse Gallop: Skip and gallop across the room and back for 10 seconds like a horse running free.

fine.description: Horse Mane: Tear a sheet of paper into long thin strips and lay them all in a row to make a mane.

hints: Say hhh-o-p. The H sound is just a warm puff of air coming out of your open mouth — try it!
Hattie huffs and puffs when she gallops — huh huh huh! Say H with every big step you take.
Uncle Ryan says: Hold your hand in front of your mouth and breathe out warm air — that warm puff is your H sound!

gross.description: Jaguar Leap: Jump forward as far as you can three times in a row like a jaguar leaping through the trees.

fine.description: Jaguar Spots: Tear a piece of paper into many small round pieces and lay them out like jaguar spots.

hints: Say jjj-a-m. Your tongue jumps up then springs back down at the start — feel that little jump in your mouth?
Joey leaps and lands — JUMP! Say J every time you jump and feel your tongue spring.
Aunt Kira says: Let your tongue jump up to the roof of your mouth and spring back down, like a little frog hopping — that happy bounce is your J sound!

gross.description: Lion Roar: Stand tall, stretch your arms out wide, and roar as loud as you can while shaking your head for 10 seconds.

fine.description: Lion Mane: Tear a sheet of paper into many strips and lay them in a big circle like a lion's fluffy mane.

hints: Say lll-i-p. Your tongue tip touches just behind your top teeth and stays up — feel that? That is L!
Leo licks his lips — lll, lll, lll! Keep your tongue tip up high and let the sound flow out.
Uncle Ryan says: Touch your tongue right behind your top teeth and hold it there while you make sound — that is your L!

gross.description: Monkey Swing: Swing both arms from side to side while walking forward for 10 seconds like a monkey swinging through trees.

fine.description: Monkey Banana: Roll a piece of paper into a long tube and bend it into a curved banana shape.

hints: Say mmm-o-p. Close your lips all the way and hum — feel that buzzy tickle? That is the M sound!
Milo hums while he swings — mmmm! Close your lips tight and let the sound buzz through your nose.
Aunt Kira says: Press your lips together softly and hum like a little lullaby — feel that warm tickle in your nose? That means you found your M!

gross.description: Newt Walk: Get on your hands and feet and walk very slowly like a newt for 10 seconds.

fine.description: Newt Tail: Tear one long, very thin strip of paper from end to end to make a newt tail.

hints: Say nnn-u-t. Your tongue touches behind your top teeth and you hum through your nose — that is N!
Ned hums as he crawls — nnn nnn nnn! Try humming with your tongue up while you walk on all fours.
Uncle Ryan says: Put your tongue behind your top teeth and hum — that nose-tickle sound is your N, and it sounds great!

gross.description: Pig Roll: Lie down on the floor and roll sideways three full times like a pig rolling in mud.

fine.description: Pig Snout: Roll a small piece of paper into a ring and tape the end down to make a round pig snout.

hints: Say ppp-i-g. Your lips press together then pop open right at the start — that pop is your P sound!
Penny pops her lips every time she oinks — P, P, P! Pop your lips just like her to say P.
Aunt Kira says: Squeeze your lips together like a tiny bubble is inside, then let it pop — that little burst of air is a lovely P sound!

gross.description: Rabbit Hop: Jump forward with both feet together eight times as fast as you can.

fine.description: Rabbit Ears: Fold a piece of paper in half lengthwise and fold up two tall points at the top to make a pair of rabbit ears.

hints: Say rrr-u-n. Pull your tongue back so it does not touch anything and make a growly sound — that is R!
Rosie makes a little growl when she runs — rrr! Pull your tongue way back and try that growl.
Uncle Ryan says: Pull your tongue back so it floats in the air and say R — that growly floating sound is perfect!

gross.description: Tiger Pounce: Crouch down as low as you can and then jump forward as far as you can two times.

fine.description: Tiger Stripes: Tear a sheet of paper into five long, thin strips and lay them out in a row like tiger stripes.

hints: Say ttt-i-p. Your tongue taps the top of your mouth very quick and sharp — tap, tap! That is T!
Tara taps her paws when she sneaks — tap, tap, tap! Say T with each tap and feel your tongue.
Aunt Kira says: Give the top of your mouth a quick, light tap with your tongue, like a tiny woodpecker — that crisp little tap is your T!

gross.description: Viper Dart: Stand still and shoot your head forward and pull it back quickly five times like a striking snake.

fine.description: Viper Tongue: Tear a thin strip of paper and then tear a small V-shape notch at one end to make a forked tongue.

hints: Say vvv-a-n. Put your top teeth on your bottom lip and turn your voice on — feel it buzz? That is V!
Vince buzzes when he darts — vvvv! Make your lip buzz just like a tiny motor when you say V.
Uncle Ryan says: Rest your top teeth on your lip and hum — that buzzy feeling on your lip means you have a great V sound!

gross.description: Wolf Howl: Tilt your head all the way back and howl at the ceiling while turning slowly in a full circle for 10 seconds.

fine.description: Wolf Paw: Press your open hand flat on a sheet of paper and use a finger to trace all the way around it to make a paw print.

hints: Say www-e-b. Round your lips into a tiny circle like you are blowing on hot soup — that round shape makes W!
Wally rounds his lips to howl — wooooo! Make that same tiny round lip shape when you say W.
Aunt Kira says: Shape your lips into a soft little circle, like you are about to blow a kiss — that cozy round shape is your W sound!

gross.description: Fox Trot: Tiptoe as quietly as you can all the way across the room and back in 10 seconds.

fine.description: Fox Tail: Roll a piece of paper into a thick, fluffy cone shape and tape the edge to make a bushy fox tail.

hints: Say f-o-ks slow. Hear that sneaky sound at the end? It is two sounds smooshed into one — K then S!
Felix has a secret at the end of his name — KS! Say fox and listen very close to that tricky ending.
Uncle Ryan says: Say fox real slow and sit on that ending — foKSSS. Two sounds hiding at the end — you found them!

gross.description: Yak Shake: Shake your head from side to side as fast as you can for 10 seconds like a yak in a big wind.

fine.description: Yak Horns: Roll two small pieces of paper into curved cone shapes to make a pair of big yak horns.

hints: Say yyy-a-k. Your tongue starts up high in your mouth then drops down fast — feel that slide? That is Y!
Yogi yells and his tongue slides down — yyy! Start high and let your tongue drop when you say Y.
Aunt Kira says: Start with your tongue up high and let it slide down gently, like going down a little hill — that smooth slide is your Y sound!

gross.description: Zebra Gallop: Gallop with your knees up high like a fast zebra for 10 seconds.

fine.description: Zebra Stripes: Tear a sheet of paper into many thin strips and line them up in a neat row like zebra stripes.

hints: Say zzz-i-p. Make your voice buzz like a little bee — keep that buzz going and that is your Z sound!
Zara zooms and buzzes — zzz! Say Z and feel your whole mouth buzz just like a tiny bee.
Uncle Ryan says: Turn on your voice and let it buzz long — zzzz. Feel that buzz in your mouth? That is a perfect Z!

gross.description: Cheetah Sprint: Run in place as fast as you possibly can for 10 seconds like the fastest animal alive.

fine.description: Cheetah Spots: Tear a piece of paper into many tiny little round bits and dot them all over another sheet like cheetah spots.

hints: Say ch-i-n. Your tongue goes up and then a big puff of air shoots out — like a tiny sneeze! That is CH!
Chase goes CH-CH-CH when she runs super fast! Say CH every time you pump your legs.
Aunt Kira says: Push your tongue up and let a warm puff of air fly out, like a little kitten sneeze — that is your CH sound!

gross.description: Shark Swim: Lie on your tummy with your arms at your sides and wiggle your whole body forward like a shark for 10 seconds.

fine.description: Shark Fin: Fold a piece of paper into a tall, stiff triangle shape to make a shark fin standing up.

hints: Say sh-i-p. Put one finger to your lips and go shhhh — that soft hush sound is your SH!
Shelly is very quiet when she swims — shhhh! Say SH nice and soft so no one can hear you coming.
Uncle Ryan says: Put your finger to your lips and shush the whole room — that hush sound is your SH and it is great!

gross.description: Thrush Flap: Flap both arms up and down like bird wings while hopping in place for 10 seconds.

fine.description: Thrush Nest: Slowly scrunch a whole sheet of paper into a round bowl shape to make a little bird nest.

hints: Say th-i-n. Stick your tongue out just a tiny bit between your teeth and blow soft air — that is TH!
Theo the thrush sticks his tongue out to sing TH! Try it — tongue peeking out, then blow gently.
Aunt Kira says: Let your tongue peek out just a tiny bit between your teeth and blow soft as a whisper — that gentle tickle is your TH sound!

gross.description: Blowfish Puff: Puff your cheeks out full of air, spread your arms as wide as you can, and spin in a slow circle for 10 seconds.

fine.description: Blowfish Body: Use both hands to crumple a whole sheet of paper into the biggest, roundest ball you can make.

hints: Say bl-o-b. Start with a lip pop — B — then slide right into L without stopping. BL, BL, BL!
Blinky goes B then the air flows right into L — no stopping in the middle! Say BL as one fast sound.
Uncle Ryan says: Pop your lips for B and keep going right into L without a break — BL together is the move!

gross.description: Bronco Kick: Lean forward with hands on your knees and kick one foot back five times fast like a wild horse bucking.

fine.description: Bronco Mane: Tear a sheet of paper into thick, wavy strips and pile them all together to make a big wild mane.

hints: Say br-i-m. Pop your lips for B and then growl an R right away — BR, BR! No pause in the middle!
Bruno goes BRRR when he bucks! Pop your lips then let the growly R come out fast right after.
Aunt Kira says: Pop your lips and let a warm little growl come right after, like a friendly bear waking up — that is your BR sound!

gross.description: Trout Flip: Lie on your tummy and lift both arms and legs off the floor at the same time five times like a fish jumping.

fine.description: Trout Scales: Tear a piece of paper into many small oval pieces and lay them in rows that overlap like fish scales.

hints: Say tr-i-p. Your tongue taps for T then growls into R right away — tap and growl, no stopping, TR!
Trixie splashes with a TR — her tail taps the water then makes a growly wave. Say TR as one fast sound!
Uncle Ryan says: Tap for T and roll right into R with no break at all — TR! That fast jump from tap to growl is awesome!
